notes: Twentieth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school uses the community as a classroom to provide personalized, engaging, and authentic learning experiences.
Official materials say the school opened in 2015.
The school says challenge-based courses called Quests help students collaborate on age-appropriate real-world problems.
Official announcements say the school combined three campuses into one permanent facility on Suttle Avenue for the 2023-24 year.
A national Canopy project selected the school for 2024-25 to showcase student-centered learning and innovation.
Official pages list Krista Lewis-Johnson as board chair and Dr. Mary Moss as founder and executive director.
Its mission says students should think with discernment and ingenuity, communicate with empathy and tolerance, and act with integrity and purpose.
Official materials say the school has been serving students and families for 18 years.
The school says it offers no school on Fridays as part of its model.
Its MTSS page says the school believes in meeting students where they are and taking them where they need to be.
Official school pages describe it as primarily a college and career preparatory school that helps students discover their own particular genius.
Official pages list JoHannah Miller as head of school and Monique Stubbs as interim chair of the board.
Official materials say integrity, responsibility, and service to others are hallmarks of its graduates.
The school says it added another division in 2017 to help students prepare for the demands of its college preparatory program.
Official news says the school earned College Board AP School Honor Roll recognition with Platinum Distinction for the 2024-25 school year.
A 2025 school news story says a senior launched the campus's first-ever National Beta Club chapter.
Official news says the school invested from its founding in a full-time college counselor in addition to a traditional school counselor.
Official pages list Stephen Chambers as board chairman, and school news identifies Melissa Smith as principal and Helen Nance as chief administrative officer.
Its mission says the school offers a challenging, character-based education in partnership with parents and community.
Official materials say the school opened in 2002.
The school highlights National Heritage Academies' Moral Focus program and says 93% of parents see a positive impact from it.
Its advanced learning program is called EXCEL-ERATE.
Official materials say the school emphasizes academic excellence, student responsibility, parent partnership, and a structured setting.
Official leadership pages list Krista Tolchin as principal and Ted Biggers as board president.
Its mission says the school provides a differentiated, challenging, and equitable learning environment for highly gifted children from diverse backgrounds.
Official materials say the school was established in 2000.
The school says its guiding principle is supporting the distinctive intellectual, social, and emotional needs of gifted children in a noncompetitive environment.
Its curriculum materials say highly gifted children not only learn faster, but also learn in a qualitatively different way.
Official materials say the community values diversity, collaboration, and vibrancy alongside respect, appreciation, trust, innovation, altruism, and creativity.
Official materials list Jessica Cuneo as interim executive director and Enrique Alvarez as board chair.
